title: Faulty Trie Deletion with Prefix Auto Completion

statement.markdown:
Given a non-empty string `prefix` and a `root` node of a Trie, delete all keys that have `prefix` as a prefix. Implement this function in the given code. The function should return `true` if the Trie is empty after deletion, `false` otherwise.

Note: The Trie data structure is implemented in the given code. You should not modify it.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the Trie is empty after deletion
statement.notes:
Time complexity: O(|prefix| + |Trie|) where |prefix| is the length of the prefix and |Trie| is the number of nodes in the Trie.

Space complexity: O(1)

examples.explanation: Deletion of key 'a' without prefix 'a' doesn't empty the Trie
Deletion of key 'ab' without prefix 'ab' doesn't empty the Trie
Deletion of key 'a' with prefix 'a' empties the Trie

hints.text: First hint text: Consider handling the case when the prefix is an empty string

editorial.title: Faulty Trie Deletion with Prefix Auto Completion
editorial.intuition: We should delete all keys that have the given prefix as a prefix using a recursive approach.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to traverse the Trie from the root node and delete all nodes that have the prefix as a prefix.
editorial.optimalSolution: To optimize the solution, we can use a recursive approach to traverse the Trie and delete all nodes that have the given prefix as a prefix. We should also handle the case when the prefix is an empty string.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: If the given prefix is 'a', we should delete the node 'a' in the Trie. Then if the Trie is empty, we should return True; otherwise, we should return False.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not handling the case when the prefix is an empty string
Mistake 2: Not deleting all nodes that have the prefix as a prefix
